description: Unified observability dashboard: security/WAF, latency percentiles, traffic, Nginx and infrastructure health.

panels.description: Distribution of total requests across routes over the selected time window.
panels.title: Requests per Route

panels.description: Requests per second grouped by HTTP status code.
panels.title: RPS by Status Code

panels.description: Requests per second split by service and route.
panels.title: RPS per Service / Route

panels.description: Percentage of requests blocked by Coraza WAF (HTTP 403) over total requests.
panels.title: WAF Block Rate (%)

panels.description: Rate of authentication failures per route. Spikes may indicate brute-force or misconfigured JWT.
panels.title: Auth Failures (401/sec)

panels.description: Rate of throttled requests per route. Requires limit-count or limit-req plugin.
panels.title: Rate Limit Hits (429/sec)

panels.description: End-to-end request latency percentiles including APISIX + upstream processing time.
panels.title: Request Latency (p90 / p95 / p99)

panels.description: Time spent inside APISIX (plugins, routing, WAF) excluding upstream response time.
panels.title: APISIX Latency (p90 / p95 / p99)

panels.description: Time spent waiting for the upstream backend to respond.
panels.title: Upstream Latency (p90 / p95 / p99)

panels.description: Median end-to-end request latency. Reflects the typical user experience.
panels.title: Request Latency (p50)

panels.description: Median time spent inside APISIX. Useful for detecting WAF/plugin overhead.
panels.title: APISIX Latency (p50)

panels.description: Median upstream response time. Baseline for backend health.
panels.title: Upstream Latency (p50)

panels.description: p99 upstream latency per node. Reveals worst-case backend response times.
panels.title: Upstream Latency p99 per Upstream

panels.description: p95 latency broken down by upstream target. Identifies which backend is the bottleneck.
panels.title: Upstream Latency p95 per Upstream

panels.description: Median latency per upstream. Compare httpbin vs crapi response times.
panels.title: Upstream Latency p50 per Upstream

panels.description: Total ingress + egress bandwidth across all routes (bytes/sec).
panels.title: Total Bandwidth

panels.description: Inbound bytes per second split by service and route.
panels.title: Ingress per Service / Route

panels.description: Outbound bytes per second split by service and route. High egress may signal large payloads.
panels.title: Egress per Service / Route

panels.description: Per-route request throughput measured by Nginx.
panels.title: Request Rate per Route (req/sec)

panels.description: Total TCP connections accepted by Nginx since last restart.
panels.title: Accepted Connections

panels.description: Total TCP connections successfully handled. Should equal Accepted Connections.
panels.title: Handled Connections

panels.description: Active / reading / writing / waiting connections over time.
panels.title: Nginx Connection State

panels.description: Tracks configuration changes in etcd (routes, upstreams, consumers, etc.).
panels.title: Etcd Modify Indexes

panels.description: 1 = etcd is reachable from APISIX. 0 = connectivity issue.
panels.title: Etcd Reachable

panels.description: Number of errors encountered while scraping Nginx metrics. Should stay at 0.
panels.title: Nginx Metric Errors

title: Apache APISIX — API Gateway Security & Performance
